_comment: Declarative workflow definition for skill-health. Diagnoses the TOS ecosystem by scanning skills/engines for readiness, reading the audit trail for recurring problems, running impact analysis for changes, and generating an ordered repair plan for human approval.
description: Ecosystem diagnostics: scan readiness → diagnose audit trail → impact analysis → repair plan (human-approved) → quality gate

steps.note: Sweep every skill (MAINTAINER.md, synced refs, evals, routing) + every shared engine (importable) + routing integrity. Emit readiness score + band + blocking issues.
Read audit trail and summarize recurring problems in plain language: validation failures, connector failure classes, minority reports.

steps.condition: when a skill is being added or renamed
steps.note: List every file that must update: routing.json, ROUTING_MODEL.md, routing-map.md, STATE.md, METRICS.md, artifact-types.json
Ordered severity-tagged plan: each step marked mechanical (safe to automate) or judgment (human-only). Stop for human approval before applying.

note: skill-health governs the SYSTEM, not classroom artifacts. Nothing high-stakes is auto-applied — repair plan is advisory; skill-repair consumes the approved plan.
